mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate keeps discount rates and financing costs low, supporting equity multiples and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when policy stays near zero without an upward path.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with stable or falling FEDFUNDS fail to deliver above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign turns negative in more than 55% of such instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPIAUCSL acceleration above recent trend signals rising inflation pressure, which lifts expected future policy rates and compresses equity valuation multiples, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above the prior 12-month average by at least 0.5pp
regime_hint: inflation scare or policy normalization regime
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-breakout episodes are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction with FEDFUNDS does not improve predictive accuracy versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, which reduces forward earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over a quarter. The effect is strongest when payroll growth is also slowing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment rise events are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if adding PAYEMS does not outperform UNRATE-only signal in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained improvement in RSAFS captures stronger retail demand and higher revenue growth for consumer-facing firms, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is valid when sales growth is broad-based rather than a one-off spike.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month retail-sales uptrends do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns more often than the unconditional baseline, or if the effect disappears after one-month forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO combined with firm HOUST indicates real-side capacity expansion and improving cyclical activity, which should lift equity earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous industrial production and housing strength fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 relative to prior quarters signals broad demand softening and lower expected corporate revenues, which should pressure S&P 500 returns lower over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth turns negative versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if negative quarterly GDP growth is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the rolling rank correlation stays near zero across recessionary windows.

mechanism_narrative: A low and falling PAYEMS trend signals labor-market resilience, supporting consumption and profits, and should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week when unemployment is not rising. The effect is a short-horizon continuation signal from labor strength into equities.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE is flat or lower
failure_condition: Reject if payroll gains with stable or falling unemployment do not produce above-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the signal reverses in soft-landing periods over a 2-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above the neutral post-crisis floor and CPIAUCSL remains elevated, the market should price a tighter discount-rate path and lower future margins, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. This mechanism fails if inflation cools while rates stay high.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 0.25% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above 2.5% year over year
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate/high-inflation regimes do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns more frequently than baseline, or if the sign becomes positive after controlling for CPIAUCSL alone.
